heading: Regulations and reports by Board
label: 4008 Regulations and reports by Board

Exceptions in Areas Where Major Disaster Exists, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–18, title V, § 50002111 Stat. 211June 12, 1997“(a).—Truth in Lending ActDuring the 240-day period beginning on the date of enactment of this Act [], the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System may make exceptions to the Truth in Lending Act [ et seq.] for transactions within an area in which the President, pursuant to section 401 of the Robert T. Stafford Disaster Relief and Emergency Assistance Act [], has determined, on or after , that a major disaster exists, or within an area determined to be eligible for disaster relief under other Federal law by reason of damage related to the 1997 flooding of the Red River of the North, the Minnesota River, and the tributaries of such rivers, if the Board determines that the exception can reasonably be expected to alleviate hardships to the public resulting from such disaster that outweigh possible adverse effects.June 12, 1997February 28, 199715 U.S.C. 160142 U.S.C. 5170“(b).—Expedited Funds Availability ActDuring the 240-day period beginning on the date of enactment of this Act [], the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System may make exceptions to the Expedited Funds Availability Act [ et seq.] for depository institution offices located within any area referred to in subsection (a) of this section if the Board determines that the exception can reasonably be expected to alleviate hardships to the public resulting from such disaster that outweigh possible adverse effects.June 12, 199712 U.S.C. 4001“(c).—Time Limit on ExceptionsAny exception made under this section shall expire not later than .September 1, 1998“(d).—Publication RequiredThe Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System shall publish in the Federal Register a statement that—“(1)describes any exception made under this section; and“(2)explains how the exception can reasonably be expected to produce benefits to the public that outweigh possible adverse effects.”Similar provisions were contained in the following prior acts:, , .Pub. L. 103–76, § 2107 Stat. 752Aug. 12, 1993, , .Pub. L. 102–485, § 3106 Stat. 2772Oct. 23, 1992

children.text: to carry out the provisions of this chapter;
to prevent the circumvention or evasion of such provisions; and
to facilitate compliance with such provisions.

children.heading: Regulations relating to improvement of check processing system
children.label: b Regulations relating to improvement of check processing system

children.text: depository institutions be charged based upon notification that a check or similar instrument will be presented for payment;
the Federal Reserve banks and depository institutions provide for check truncation;
depository institutions be provided incentives to return items promptly to the depository institution of first deposit;
the Federal Reserve banks and depository institutions take such actions as are necessary to automate the process of returning unpaid checks;

children.label: A
children.text: place its endorsement, and other notations specified in regulations of the Board, on checks in the positions specified in such regulations; and

children.label: i
children.text: automate the process of reading endorsements; and

children.label: ii
children.text: eliminate unnecessary endorsements;

children.label: A
children.text: such originating depository institution determines whether it will pay such check; and

children.label: B
children.text: if such originating depository institution determines that it will not pay such check, such originating depository institution directly notify the receiving depository institution of such determination;
regardless of where a check is cleared initially, all returned checks be eligible to be returned through the Federal Reserve System;

Federal Reserve banks and depository institutions participate in the development and implementation of an electronic clearinghouse process to the extent the Board determines, pursuant to the study under subsection (f), that such a process is feasible; and
originating depository institutions be permitted to return unpaid checks directly to, and obtain reimbursement for such checks directly from, the receiving depository institution.

children.heading: Regulatory responsibility of Board for payment system
children.label: c Regulatory responsibility of Board for payment system
A
children.text: any aspect of the payment system, including the receipt, payment, collection, or clearing of checks; and

children.label: B
children.text: any related function of the payment system with respect to checks.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 2 Regulations
children.text: The Board shall prescribe such regulations as it may determine to be appropriate to carry out its responsibility under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Required reports
children.label: A Required reports
children.text: The Board shall transmit a report to both Houses of the Congress not later than 18, 30, and 48 months after .August 10, 1987

children.label: i
children.text: the actions taken and progress made by the Board to implement the schedules established in , andsection 4002 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the impact of this chapter on consumers and depository institutions.

children.heading: Report required
children.label: A Report required
children.text: The Board shall transmit a report to both Houses of the Congress not later than 2 years after , regarding the effects the temporary schedule established under have had on depository institutions and the public.August 10, 1987section 4002(c) of this title

children.heading: Contents of report
children.label: B Contents of report
children.text: Such report shall also assess the potential impact the implementation of the schedule established in will have on depository institutions and the public, including an estimate of the risks to and losses of depository institutions and the benefits to consumers. Such report shall also contain such recommendations for legislative or administrative action as the Board may determine to be necessary.section 4002(b) of this title

children.heading: Comptroller General evaluation report
children.label: 3 Comptroller General evaluation report
children.text: Not later than 6 months after , the Comptroller General of the United States shall transmit a report to the Congress evaluating the implementation and administration of this chapter.September 1, 1988

children.heading: Consultations
children.label: e Consultations
children.text: In prescribing regulations under subsections (a) and (b), the Board and the Director of the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection, in the case of subsection (a), and the Board, in the case of subsection (b), shall consult with the Comptroller of the Currency, the Board of Directors of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation, and the National Credit Union Administration Board.

children.heading: Study required
children.label: 1 Study required
children.text: The Board shall study the feasibility of modernizing and accelerating the check payment system through the development of an electronic clearinghouse process utilizing existing telecommunications technology to avoid the necessity of actual presentment of the paper instrument to a payor institution before such institution is charged for the item.

children.label: A
children.text: consult with appropriate experts in telecommunications technology; and

children.label: B
children.text: consider all practical and legal impediments to the development of an electronic clearinghouse process.

children.heading: Report required
children.label: 3 Report required
children.text: The Board shall report its conclusions to the Congress within 9 months of .August 10, 1987
